name: Passport: Expedite Request, Appointment Booking, or Lost-Passport Report
description: Call the National Passport Information Center (NPIC) or a Regional Passport Agency to book an urgent in-person appointment, escalate an in-flight application, report a lost or stolen passport, or get a status update beyond what the online tracker shows.
disclaimer: Not legal or immigration advice. For dual-citizenship, visa, or admissibility questions, refer to the receiving country's consulate or an immigration attorney. The agent will not advise on whether to travel or how to handle international entry/exit issues.

context.when_to_use: Travel is within 14 calendar days (life-or-death = within 72 hours) and the user needs an in-person Regional Passport Agency appointment. Online status hasn't updated in > 2 weeks past the published processing window. Passport was lost or stolen and the user needs both a replacement plan and a fraud report.
preconditions: User has the application locator number (if one was issued).
User has proof of imminent travel: itinerary, ticket, or e-ticket confirmation (PDF in hand).
Photo ID (driver's license or other government ID).
If life-or-death case: documentation of the family emergency (hospital letter, death certificate, mortuary contact).
Two callback options — NPIC frequently disconnects mid-call.

principles: NPIC = 1-877-487-2778. Hours are Mon–Fri 8am–10pm ET, Sat 10am–3pm ET. Call AT OPENING for the lightest queue.
Regional Passport Agency in-person appointments open on a rolling basis 2 weeks before travel. The pool refreshes — keep redialing or have the agent watch for slots if the first attempt has none.
Life-or-death emergencies (death, serious illness/injury) go through a special line (1-202-647-4000 after-hours) — the standard NPIC line will NOT take these efficiently.
Expedite by mail (≈ 5–7 weeks at time of writing) is NOT the same as an in-person Regional Agency appointment (same-day to 2 weeks). Don't conflate them.
Lost-or-stolen passports must be reported (DS-64) and that report INVALIDATES the lost document — only file if you've truly looked everywhere.

phrases.opener_appointment: Hi, I need an in-person appointment at the Regional Passport Agency. I'm traveling on [DATE], which is in [N] days. My application locator is [LOCATOR] (if applicable). Can you check what's available?
phrases.opener_status: Hi, I'm calling about application locator [LOCATOR]. The online tracker still shows [STATUS] and we're past the published [routine/expedited] window. Can you check the file?
phrases.opener_life_or_death: Hi, this is a life-or-death emergency. My [relationship] is [seriously ill / has died] and I need to travel internationally within [HOURS]. I have [documentation type] ready to send. What's the next step?
phrases.opener_lost: Hi, I need to report a lost passport and request a replacement. The passport number is [NUMBER], issued [DATE]. I've completed Form DS-64. What's the fastest way to get a replacement given that I [do / do not] have imminent travel?
phrases.ask_for_agency_transfer: Could you transfer me to the [CITY] Regional Passport Agency directly? I want to see if they have any walk-in or same-day appointments.
phrases.stall_documents: Give me one moment to bring up my itinerary.
phrases.graceful_close: Thank you. Can I confirm the appointment date/time, the office address, the exact documents I need to bring, the fee, and a confirmation number — plus your name and ID?

tactics.name: Triage the urgency level
tactics.script: Bucket: (a) life-or-death (< 72h, family emergency) → special line; (b) urgent travel (3–14 days) → Regional Agency in-person; (c) expedited (2–8 weeks) → mail-in expedite + courier; (d) routine. Route appropriately — wrong bucket = wrong line = wasted hour.

tactics.name: For urgent travel, ask for ANY Regional Agency, not just the closest
tactics.when: User can travel domestically to get the passport.
tactics.script: If LA has no slots and Houston does, and the user can fly to Houston, that may beat waiting. Some Agencies serve walk-ins; some are appointment-only.

tactics.name: Confirm the in-person doc list
tactics.script: Standard: DS-11 (new) or DS-82 (renewal), proof of citizenship (certified birth cert or prior passport), photo ID + photocopy, passport photo (2×2 inches), travel itinerary, payment for fees + expedite. Walking in without one of these = appointment wasted.

tactics.name: For lost passports, file DS-64 even if travel is imminent
tactics.script: Without DS-64 on file, the new passport process is stalled. File the report, then immediately move to the urgent-travel in-person appointment flow.

tactics.name: Document every reference number
tactics.script: Locator, appointment confirmation, agent name + ID, case number for status escalations. These are gold for follow-up if the call drops.

boundaries: Do NOT advise on whether the user can enter a destination country (visa, six-month-validity rule, entry stamps) — refer to the destination consulate or IATA Timatic.
Do NOT promise an appointment can be obtained — slots are real-time and limited.
Do NOT pay the expedite fee or appointment fee without explicit user authorization.
Do NOT file DS-64 on speculation — it invalidates the passport. Confirm the user has searched thoroughly.
Do NOT engage with anyone claiming to sell expedited appointments outside the official channels — common scam.
success_signals: Appointment is booked at a named Regional Agency, with date/time + doc list captured.
Application status escalation produces a movement (passport mailed / new estimated date).
Lost passport report (DS-64) is on file and a replacement path is scheduled.
Life-or-death case is moved to the appropriate special-handling team.
failure_signals: No Regional Agency has any appointments within travel window — escalate to congressional rep / senator's caseworker (legitimate channel for travel emergencies).
Status escalation produces no information beyond what the online tracker shows.
NPIC line disconnects > 2 times — try first-thing morning or Saturday.
User is being asked to pay an unfamiliar third party — likely scam; redirect to travel.state.gov.

exit_strategy.on_success: Capture: appointment date/time/office/address, document list, fee, confirmation number, NPIC agent name + ID. Repeat doc list back to the user. Send the user the official Regional Agency address and a what-to-bring checklist.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Capture the obstacle, advise the user on the congressional-rep escalation path (most US Senators' offices have a passport caseworker), and confirm next NPIC-callback window.
follow_ups: Calendar mail-out estimate; if not received, call NPIC with the locator.
If congressional escalation was used, follow up with the caseworker after 48 hours.
If passport was lost, calendar a check that DS-64 invalidation propagated (check Online Status Check).

required_user_info: Full legal name, DOB, place of birth, current passport number (if any)
Type of request: new / renewal / lost-replacement / name change
Documents in hand (birth cert, prior passport, photo ID, passport photo)
Payment method authorized for fees + expedite
Whether the user can travel domestically to a Regional Agency
hang_up_and_call_back_signals: NPIC line disconnected mid-call — call back immediately; the locator preserves your progress.
Rep cannot find the application by locator — verify locator on the user's online account first; possibly a typo.
Suspect scam offer for paid 'expedite appointments' — hang up; only book via travel.state.gov or NPIC.
If at 7+ days from travel with no Regional Agency slot — escalate to congressional caseworker (Senator's state office), then re-engage NPIC.
